other_observers: Joey Kellner, Glenn Walbek, Joe Roller and Loch Kilpatrick.; Steve Mlodinow and Tim Smart found the bird. Now seen by many others; eg Bill Maynard, and Mark Peterson.
specific_location: On an island in the middle of the South Platte River just east of Hwy 385 and just south of Julesburg.
description: A very small dove; much smaller than Mourning Dove and more like the size of a largish sparrow. The head and hind neck were gray with some scaling. The face was slightly reddish; the eye looked dark and so did the bill. The upperparts, including the very short tail, were a uniform dark brown/gray. The wing coverts were also brownish, but with distinct black spots. The breast was also quite heavily scaled. The legs and feet were a quite bright shade of red. When the bird flew, the outer half of the wings showed much deep red with some black, and the outer tail showed black feathers.Behaviors: Flying between the ground and small trees, trying not to be photographed by the people chasing it.Call: None heard.Plumage: Winter
